name: Unreal AI ControlRig
description: An AI Game Developer (Unreal-MCP) extension contributing MCP tools an AI agent can call against the Unreal Editor.

tools.name: control-rig-list
tools.description: Lists every Control Rig blueprint (UControlRigBlueprint) asset in the project via the Asset Registry, without loading any of them. Optionally filter by a content-path prefix. Returns { count, controlRigs:[{ name, path }] }.

tools.name: control-rig-get
tools.description: Inspects a single Control Rig blueprint asset (read-only) and reports its rig element hierarchy. Returns { path, name, boneCount, nullCount, controlCount, curveCount, elementCount, elements:[{ name, type }] }.

tools.name: control-rig-list-controls
tools.description: Lists the control elements of a Control Rig blueprint (read-only), each with its control type (e.g. Float, Transform, Bool). Returns { path, controlCount, controls:[{ name, controlType }] }.

tools.name: control-rig-list-bones
tools.description: Lists the bone elements of a Control Rig blueprint (read-only), each with its immediate parent bone (empty for a root). Returns { path, boneCount, bones:[{ name, parent }] }.

tools.name: control-rig-get-component
tools.description: Inspects the Control Rig component of an actor in the active editor world (read-only). Looks the actor up by its label. Returns { actorName, componentName, controlRigClass } or a defensive error if the actor or component is not found.

_comment: Install-catalog / compatibility manifest, shaped as ONE unreal-mcp-cli ExtensionDescriptor (cli/src/utils/extensions-catalog.ts in Unreal-MCP) so it is directly liftable into the shared extensions.catalog.json { schemaVersion, extensions[] }. 'version' mirrors the .uplugin VersionName (kept in sync by commands/bump-version.ps1). 'minCoreVersion' is the lowest Unreal-MCP core version this extension supports (refreshed by commands/update-core.ps1). 'enginePlugins' are the gating engine plugins (e.g. ['Niagara']) enabled in the .uproject alongside this extension — populated by commands/init -FeaturePlugin. 'tools' lists the contributed tool ids/descriptions. The install-extension resolver unions enginePlugins with the materialized .uplugin Plugins[].
